title: Fuel surcharges jump as airlines tighten schedules
desc: Local airlines have continued to reduce capacity by consolidating less profitable flights into remaining services to stay afloat amid rising jet fuel prices, while Japan Airlines and ANA announced a doubling of fuel surcharges, with Thailand-bound fuel surcharges increasing from about 3,000 baht to 6,000 baht.

title: Industrial land prices flat as Chinese investors sell up
desc: Despite strong demand, average asking prices for industrial land are expected to remain flat due to an influx of land from Chinese investors unable to lease or operate businesses on their plots, according to property consultancy Cushman & Wakefield Thailand.

title: Plastic supply dubbed stable
desc: Supplies of plastic pellets and plastic packaging are sufficient and expected to last until at least July, according to plastic pellet manufacturers, distributors, and industrial users.

title: BLS sees strong Q1 results for listed firms
desc: Listed Thai companies are on track to deliver strong first-quarter financial results, with net profits projected to rise 15% year-on-year and 26% quarter-on-quarter, underpinned by sector-specific tailwinds, says Bualuang Securities (BLS).

title: PM mulls B500bn loan bid
desc: Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul has instructed his deputy Ekniti Nitithanprapas and the Council of State to study the legal implications of issuing an emergency loan decree and expanding the country's public debt ceiling.

title: Wary response to borrowing plan
desc: The government is preparing to issue an emergency decree to borrow 500 billion baht and raise the public debt ceiling, according to a news report on Monday.

title: Flyover to close until February
desc: The busy Pratunam flyover on Phetchaburi Road will close from Friday at 10pm until next February to allow for the construction of an underground section of the Orange Line MRT, according to the Metropolitan Police Bureau (MPB) and the Mass Rapid Transit Authority of Thailand (MRTA).

title: Gasoline users left out of fuel price cut
desc: Fuel pricing policy is again under scrutiny as critics urge the government to extend benefits from falling global crude oil prices to gasoline and gasohol consumers, who they argue are disproportionately burdened by contributions to the Oil Fuel Fund.

title: Hospital and airline tycoon Prasert dies at 93
desc: Dr Prasert Prasarttong-Osoth, the founder of Thailand’s largest private hospital business as well as an airline that put Koh Samui on the global tourism map, has passed away at the age of 93.

title: Court rejects 500,000 baht bail, sends officer to jail in fatal Pattaya shooting
desc: The accused police officer, known as “Inspector Joe the Gunman,” is seen being transferred in a prison transport truck to Pattaya Special Prison after the court denied bail in a fatal Walking Street shooting case. PATTAYA, Thailand – A police officer known as “Inspector Joe the Gunman” has been sent to jail after the Pattaya Provincial Court rejected his bail request in a fatal shooting case on Walking Street. Pol Sub-Lt Jirasak Srikatthanam, 54, a member of the Pattaya City Police investigation unit, is accused of fatally shooting a 41-year-old cannabis shop owner, Pattarathon Jirachokchaikul

title: Pattaya launches massive free health screening drive for seniors ahead of April 30 event
desc: Deputy Mayor Wuthisak Rermkijakarn chairs the planning meeting as Pattaya prepares a large-scale free health screening program for seniors, aimed at expanding preventive healthcare and improving quality of life for elderly residents. PATTAYA, Thailand – Pattaya City is preparing to roll out a large-scale free health screening initiative for older residents as part of its “Healthy Living” program 2026, aiming to strengthen preventive healthcare and improve quality of life for the elderly. The preparations were chaired by Deputy Mayor Wuthisak Rermkijakarn, who presided over a planning meeting a

title: Irish man allegedly sets Pattaya condo room on fire during hallucination scare, three rescued
desc: Firefighters and rescue teams evacuate residents and rescue three people after an Irish man allegedly set fire to a condominium room in central Pattaya during an apparent hallucination episode early on April 21. PATTAYA, Thailand – An Irish man allegedly set fire to a condominium room in central Pattaya during an apparent hallucination episode early Tuesday morning, April 21, forcing emergency crews to rescue three people trapped inside the smoke-filled building. Police, firefighters, and rescue teams were called to a condominium on Central Pattaya Soi 4 at around 3:46 a.m. after reports of a

title: City pushes health upgrade drive with Pattaya Rak Center partnership
desc: Mayor Poramet Ngampichet led discussions with the Pattaya Rak preventive medicine center to strengthen healthcare services for residents and tourists, supporting efforts to enhance travel health services, vaccinations, and disease screening across the city. PATTAYA, Thailand – Pattaya City is moving to enhance its public health services through closer cooperation with the Pattaya Rak Center, a preventive medicine unit under the Department of Disease Control, Ministry of Public Health, aimed at improving healthcare access for both residents and tourists. Mayor Poramet Ngampichet on Tuesday, Apr

title: Drunk foreigner injured after falling from height near Pattaya’s Pratumnak Pier
desc: Jomtien municipal officers found an intoxicated foreign man with head injuries and abrasions after he reportedly fell from a height near a pier on Pratumnak Soi 5. PATTAYA, Thailand – A foreign man was injured after falling from a height near a pier in the Pratumnak area late Tuesday night, April 21, prompting a response from municipal officers and rescue workers. Jomtien municipal officers received reports from local residents of an intoxicated foreign man who had fallen near the Ziggy Pier area on Pratumnak Road Soi 5. Officers arrived at the scene and found the man with a head wound and min

title: Foreign tourists injured in jet ski collision off Pattaya Beach
desc: Pattaya municipal officers and lifeguards rushed to assist after two jet skis collided off Pattaya Beach, leaving foreign tourists injured and requiring ambulance transport to hospital. PATTAYA, Thailand – Several foreign tourists were injured after two jet skis collided off Pattaya Beach on Tuesday, April 21, prompting a rapid emergency response from local authorities and rescue teams. The incident occurred along Pattaya Beach, where municipal enforcement officers and lifeguards were called to assist after reports of a jet ski crash involving foreign visitors. Pattaya municipal officers quick

title: Emergency response activated after report of missing child near Pattaya shopping mall
desc: Pattaya municipal officers safely assisted a 5-year-old boy known as “Nong Pai” after he was found separated from his parents near Central Pattaya, before transferring him to the Chonburi Child and Family Protection Home under child welfare procedures. PATTAYA, Thailand – Authorities in Pattaya quickly responded to assist a 5-year-old boy who was found separated from his parents near a major shopping mall in the city, ensuring his safety before transferring him to child welfare officials. The incident occurred on April 21 after Pattaya City Radio Center coordinated with Pattaya City Police Sta

title: Moody’s upgrades Thailand outlook to ‘stable’
desc: Moody’s Ratings on Tuesday upgraded the outlook for the government of Thailand to stable from negative, while affirming the foreign and local currency issuer and local currency senior unsecured credit ratings at Baa1.

title: Thai woman’s Songkran GHB exposure claim investigated as poison centre questions theory
desc: Thai woman's Songkran GHB exposure claim investigated as poison centre questions theory Legacy Ramathibodi Poison Centre said the chances of successfully administering gamma-hydroxybutyrate, or GHB , through a water gun were very low after a Thai woman claimed she may have been exposed to the drug during Songkran celebrations in Bangkok. The woman posted a warning on TikTok on April 13, saying she was hospitalised after being exposed to GHB at an entertainment venue in the capital during Songkran.

title: 2026年全球生活成本谁最贵？泰国排第几？
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 据Visual Capitalist日前发布的《2026年全球生活成本指数》报告显示，该指数基于对全球155个国家和地区的调查，评估指标涵盖日常生活开支与住房租金两大类别。

title: 菲律宾总统邀阿努廷出席东盟峰会，肯定泰国反诈成绩
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 4月21日13时30分，泰国总理阿努廷在总理办公室，与菲律宾总统费迪南德·罗慕尔德兹·马科斯举行电话会谈。

title: Woman catches, punches peeping tom filming in Surat Thani toilet
desc: Woman catches, punches peeping tom filming in Surat Thani toilet Legacy A woman punched a man after catching him filming her inside a toilet at a petrol station in Surat Thani on April 16, before police were called .

title: Japanese chef closes ramen shop after online fame reveals permit issue
desc: Japanese chef closes ramen shop after online fame reveals permit issue Legacy A Japanese chef temporarily closed his ramen shop in Samut Prakan after a work permit issue came to light following the shop’s rise to fame on Thai social media.

title: Frigate deal puts Thai Navy on path to shipbuilding self-reliance
desc: Frigate deal puts Thai Navy on path to shipbuilding self-reliance Legacy The Royal Thai Navy held a meeting with the Thai Shipbuilding and Repairing Association (TSBA) and domestic shipyard operators yesterday, April 20.

title: Thai AirAsia X flight changes leave travellers scrambling
desc: Thai AirAsia X flight changes leave travellers scrambling Legacy Thai passengers have complained online after Thai AirAsia X (AA X) changed several Japan-bound flights and issued flight cancellations.

title: Row with boyfriend leads Thai woman to step into oncoming traffic
desc: Row with boyfriend leads Thai woman to step into oncoming traffic Legacy A woman came under criticism from Thai social media users after she was seen walking in the middle of a road in Chachoengsao at around midnight on Saturday, April 18.

title: Foreign creator falls in love with Thailand, moves to Bangkok
desc: Foreign creator falls in love with Thailand, moves to Bangkok Legacy A foreign content creator has drawn attention online after announcing she has moved to Bangkok , describing the Thai capital as her dream city.

title: 泰国警方澄清“水枪投毒”案受害者身份：系泰国籍并非中国公民
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 4月21日，针对近日网络热议的“宋干节女子疑遭高压水枪投毒”事件，泰国警方于4月20日正式发布澄清声明。声明指出，该事件受害者为泰国籍女性，并非部分网传信息所称的中国公民。

title: 王毅将于4月22日至26日应邀访问柬埔寨、泰国、缅甸
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 外交部发言人宣布：中共中央政治局委员、外交部长王毅将于4月22日至26日应邀访问柬埔寨、泰国、缅甸。访柬期间，王毅将与国防部长董军共同出席中柬外长、防长“2+2”战略对话机制首次会议。 (来源：外交部网站) The post 王毅将于4月22日至26日应邀访问柬埔寨、泰国、缅甸 appeared first on 泰国头条新闻 .

title: Russian suspect arrested in Phuket over scam cash withdrawals
desc: Russian suspect arrested in Phuket over scam cash withdrawals Legacy Police in Phuket arrested a Russian national yesterday, April 20, in connection with a call centre scam after he was found withdrawing funds linked to fraudulent operations . The suspect, identified as 41 year old Anton Kurchenko, was monitored by Phuket Provincial Police after making multiple ATM withdrawals outside a supermarket in Soi Ta-iad, Chalong subdistrict. According to a report from the Phuket Times Facebook page, Kurchenko withdrew cash 16 times, totalling around 331,000 baht. Police said the funds were linked to 1

title: Radisson Hotel Chateau de Bangkok opens in Ploen Chit after 30-year legacy
desc: Radisson Hotel Chateau de Bangkok opens in Ploen Chit after 30-year legacy Legacy Radisson Hotel Group has opened Radisson Hotel Chateau de Bangkok in Ploen Chit, Bangkok, reviving a property that has stood in Soi Ruamrudee for over 30 years. The hotel offers 178 guest rooms and suites, three dining venues, and event facilities across several dedicated spaces. The relaunched property retains the building's established three-decade character while updating interiors and modernising amenities throughout. Many rooms offer skyline views, and suites with kitchenettes are available for long-st

title: Foreigner claims 0% tax after buying 56 million baht Phuket home
desc: Foreigner claims 0% tax after buying 56 million baht Phuket home Legacy A foreign social media user has drawn attention online after claiming to have bought a 56 million baht home in Phuket and could live in Thailand long-term while paying zero tax on global income. The post, shared on X, showed the individual with his partner alongside a message stating they had signed a contract to purchase a “dream home” near Bang Tao Beach for 56 million baht, or £1.3 million. The foreign man described his Phuket home as a freehold property and said the purchase would allow him to qualify for “Wealthy Glob

title: 泰国“移动版AI人脸识别”上岗，成功抓获印度逾期男子
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 4月19日，泰国春武里府旅游警察局第一分局第二支队第四大队调查组在芭提雅展开“万莱节”违法犯罪活动打击行动，旨在提高全国主要旅游景点的安全水平。在本次行动中，警方出动搭载AI人脸识别系统的移动指挥车，在海滩沿岸道路进行巡查，并成功识别出一名35岁的印度籍男子，AI系统显示他已在泰国逾期居留43天。

title: 潜心学习中文超2年，泰女星Aokbab首演中国剧女主
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 4月21日，因主演电影《天才枪手》而被观众熟知的泰国女演员“Aokbab”近日官宣加入由何润东执导中国台湾电视剧《对你心动的预言》，并在剧中挑大梁担任女主角...

title: 春武里豪宅30名印度籍人员涉非法交易被控 月流水达3000万泰铢
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 4月20日，春武里移民局在芭提雅地区开展了突击行动，在一处高档住宅内发现印度籍人员30人，其涉嫌非法从事股票及加密货币交易业务。

title: 亚洲最富20大家族财富增幅创纪录 泰国3大家族跻身榜单前20
desc: 2026年共有3个来自泰国的家族进入“亚洲20大富豪家族”榜单，分别为排名第4位的谢氏家族、第7位的许...

title: 油价冲击叠加淡季弱需求 泰国国际航空5月取消46班次航班
desc: 受中东地区冲突引发的能源紧张影响，航空燃油成本上升，旅游淡季阶段旅客出行需求趋缓。泰国国际航空有限公司（大众）已向机票代理商通报，将在2026年5月对航班计划作出调整...
